THE TRIAL DATE IS SET FOR 2/4/08 @ 11:15 AM IN DEPT. 206; SETTLEMENT CONFERENCES SET FOR 1/24/08, 1/28/08, 1/30/08, 1/31/08 & 2/1/08 @ 10:30AM IN DEPT. 220. PLAINTIFF'S DEMAND IS DUE ON 1/18/08. PLAINTIFF SHALL DISCLOSE ALL PRODUCT IDENTIFICATION AND CAUSATION WITNESSES AS TO EACH DEFENDANT BY 12/6/07, AND SHALL ALSO PRODUCE DOCUMENTS THEY INTEND TO USE AT TRIAL AS TO PRODUCT IDENTIFICATION AS TO EACH DEFENDANT BY THAT DATE. IF PLAINTIFF LEARNS OF NEW WITNESSES, IN GOOD FAITH, THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT PROVIDED ON THE DUE DATE. DEFENDANTS TO PROVIDE INFORMATION REGARDING PROPOSITION 51 WITNESSES AND AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES BY 12/26/07. IF DEFENDANTS LEARNS OF NEW WITNESSES, IN GOOD FAITH, THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT PROVIDED ON THE DUE DATE. ALL ECONOMIC EXPERT DISCOVERY IS TO BE COMPLETED 14 CALENDAR DAYS BEFORE THE TRIAL DATE, INCLUDING DEPOSITION OF ECONOMIC EXPERT. THE PARED-DOWN LIST OF EXPERTS WILL BE FAXED NO LATER THAN 14 DAYS BEFORE TRIAL DATE. NO LATER THAN 12 DAYS BEFORE THE TRIAL DATE, EACH SIDE MAY IDENTIFY UP TO THREE EXPERT WITNESSES PERCEIVED TO BE NECESSARY TO BE DEPOSED FOR MEANINGFUL SETTLEMENT DISCUSSIONS TO OCCUR. THESE EXPERTS WILL BE DEPOSED PRIOR TO THE TRIAL DATE. TRIAL WILL NOT BE DELAYED FOR THE PURPOSE OF DEPOSING EXPERTS. WRITTEN ORDER PENDING. JUDGE: A.
